pattern: Buying a fruit unlocks a run of guaranteed-free fruits sized to how deep into the array that purchase sits, so the cheapest way to own everything is a prefix DP over which fruit you actually pay for next, letting an already-free fruit be repurchased whenever its longer reach is worth it.
hints: Every purchase unlocks a stretch of free fruits right after it, and the deeper into the array the purchased fruit is, the longer that stretch reaches -- so a fruit already obtained for free can still be worth buying again purely for a longer reach.

Build the answer prefix by prefix: the cheapest way to own the first several fruits only needs the cheapest way to own a shorter prefix, plus the price of one more purchase whose reach covers the rest of that prefix.

For each prefix length, try every earlier prefix length whose purchase would stretch far enough to finish covering it, and keep the cheapest total across all of those choices -- there is no shortcut that skips comparing several candidates.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a worthwhile double purchase
statement example, skipping the costly middle fruit
statement example, repurchasing an already-free fruit for extra reach
single fruit at the minimum price
single fruit at the maximum price
cheap first fruit frees an expensive second one
expensive first fruit is unavoidable even though the second is cheap
a second costly fruit deep in the array is worth repurchasing for its own free run

stress.description: random prices at the maximum array length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    prices = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [prices]
